# Platform Playbooks

## TikTok

### Primary signals

```text
watch time
full watch
skip avoidance
shares
comments
search/topic relevance
trend/culture fit
```

### Tactics

```text
Use Creator Search Insights weekly.
Build videos around content gaps.
Open with a cultural or problem-specific hook.
Use comments as new video prompts.
Make searchable captions, not vague captions.
Use relevant hashtags only.
Avoid duplicate/reposted-looking content.
```

### TikTok format

```text
0–1s: visual problem/result
1–3s: conversational hook
3–7s: first payoff
middle: example/proof
end: comment or send trigger
caption: exact topic phrase
```

### TikTok hypothesis

```text
Search-backed TikToks have longer tails than trend-backed TikToks.
```

Test:

```text
A: Creator Search Insights topic
B: trend sound topic
C: hybrid search topic + trend-aware packaging
```

## YouTube Shorts

### Primary signals

```text
shown in feed
chose-to-view
engaged views
average percentage viewed
average view duration
subscriber conversion
search terms
```

### Tactics

```text
Treat the first frame like a thumbnail.
Use direct titles.
Make search-friendly educational Shorts.
Track engaged views, not just public views.
Connect Shorts to longer videos/playlists when relevant.
Use stronger first-frame text than on TikTok when the topic is educational.
```

### Shorts format

```text
0–1s: thumbnail-like frame
1–3s: direct hook
3–7s: first proof/result
middle: compressed explanation
end: subscribe/series reason
```

### Shorts hypothesis

```text
First-frame changes produce measurable differences in chose-to-view.
```

Test:

```text
same body, five first-frame variants.
```

## Instagram Reels

### Primary signals

```text
average watch time
sends/shares per reach
saves per reach
comments
non-follower performance
profile visits
```

### Tactics

```text
Use Trial Reels for new formats.
Bias toward sendable identity content.
Make CTAs recipient-specific.
Use clean, native-feeling visuals.
Test non-follower hooks separately from follower hooks.
Avoid repost/watermark aesthetics.
```

### Reels format

```text
0–1s: relatable visual problem
1–3s: identity hook
3–10s: useful proof/fix
middle: compressed example
end: send/save CTA
```

### Reels hypothesis

```text
Recipient-specific send CTAs outperform generic “share this” prompts.
```

Test:

```text
A: no CTA
B: “share this”
C: “send this to someone editing Reels”
D: “send this to someone whose captions cover the subject”
```
